en.extract:
The cinnamon weaver (Ploceus badius) is a species of bird in the family Ploceidae. It is found only in Sudan and South Sudan.
The cinnamon weaver is nomadic, returning to breeding areas during the September–October breeding season. Possibly monogamous, weavers live communally, preferring tall trees with dense foliage.

The '''cinnamon weaver''' ('''''Ploceus badius''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Ploceidae]]. It is found only in Sudan and South Sudan.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />
The cinnamon weaver is nomadic, returning to breeding areas during the September–October breeding season. Possibly monogamous, weavers live communally, preferring tall trees with dense foliage.<ref>[http://www.hbw.com/species/cinnamon-weaver-ploceus-badius 'Handbook of the Birds of the World'], Retrieved 26 Feb 2016</ref>
